_notes: WP-187 / EC-214 — curated, human-reviewed [effect:] marker map. WP-188 / EC-215 extends it to admit the Escape:/Overrun: timings. Consumed by apply-effect-markers.mjs, which appends [effect:<VillainEffectKeyword>] tokens to the matched Ambush:/Fight:/Escape:/Overrun: ability line in data/cards/*.json. WP-185's engine setup parser reads the Ambush/Fight markers; WP-186's parser reads the Escape/Overrun markers (collapsing both prefixes to the single onEscape timing engine-side).

Keys: villains -> setAbbr -> villainGroupSlug -> villainCardSlug -> { ambush?, fight?, escape?, overrun? }; henchmen -> setAbbr -> henchmanGroupSlug -> { ambush?, fight?, escape?, overrun? } (henchman ability text is group-level). Each list holds only the five locked VillainEffectKeyword strings: gainWoundEachPlayer, gainWoundCurrentPlayer, koHeroCurrentPlayer, heroDeckTopToEscape, captureBystander.

v1 curation discipline (WP-185 semantics): a line is marked ONLY when its entire effect reduces to unconditional + magnitude-1 + single-target applications of the MVP vocabulary. Conditional (may / If / '... or ...' choice), magnitude>1 (two / all / 'for each'), wrong/multi target (each villain, the Mastermind, another player, target-restricted KO), and lines carrying an extra non-MVP clause ('... Then ...') are deliberately left unmarked. Conservatism over coverage.

WP-188 curatable Escape shapes: ONLY 'Escape: Each player gains a Wound.' plus punctuation-/markup-only variants — this is gainWoundEachPlayer's first data (zero matches under WP-187, which found no unconditional each-player wound lines on Ambush/Fight). DEFER anything with 'If'/'When'/'Unless'/'For each', '... or ...' choices, magnitude>1, compound '(After the normal Escape KO) ...', or 'each other player' wording.

WP-188 'overrun' curation: the gate is widened so --propose scans overrun and _unassigned can capture it, but ZERO overrun entries were curatable under v1 discipline. Villain/henchman overrun lines appear only in the standalone Fear-Itself / Villains-Only sets and all carry either conditional choices, '[keyword:Demolish]' (no MVP keyword), or 'Bindings' (Fear-Itself recoloring distinct from 'Wound'). An empty overrun curation set is a valid v1 outcome per WP-188 §v1 Curation, not a gap. Scheme overrun is explicitly out of scope.

_unassigned reason vocabulary (closed set; the script does NOT read this field — it is human-review documentation): magnitude>1 | multi-target | other | conditional | multi-line (WP-187 set) + no-vocabulary-keyword (WP-188; first-class, NOT folded under 'other'). 'no-vocabulary-keyword' names every literal 'Each player KOs ...' villain Escape/Overrun line that has no keyword in the current vocabulary; D-18802 records the deferral. WP-190 (drafted, commit 1ac0762) reads exactly these rows to promote the unconditional magnitude-1 subset to [effect:koHeroEachPlayer] once WP-189 adds the keyword. This tagging is a cross-WP contract, not internal bookkeeping — the no-vocabulary-keyword rows below are EXHAUSTIVE across the 40 sets; rows for the other reason values remain representative per the WP-187 precedent.

The exhaustive candidate list is reproducible at any time via `node scripts/convert-cards/apply-effect-markers.mjs --propose`. WP-187 surfaced 221 Ambush/Fight phrase-match candidates (2026-05-28); WP-188 widening surfaced an additional 57 Escape/Overrun candidates (2026-05-29). The _unassigned block below records the human classification RATIONALE with representative rows per reason (plus EVERY 'Each player KOs' Escape line, exhaustively); it is intentionally not a 1:1 copy of --propose output.

DATA-vs-SPEC drift found during WP-187 execution (2026-05-28; unchanged by WP-188): (a) The WP-187 spec assumed two cards (one bkwd, one rvlt) carry two Fight: lines apiece and would loud-fail as reason 'multi-line'. Under the LOCKED predicate (line.trimStart() begins with 'Fight:'), NO card has two timing lines of the same timing — rvlt/army-of-evil/mister-hyde and rvlt/dark-avengers/sentry each have a SECOND 'Fight:' embedded mid-line (inside flavor / a granted ability), which the predicate correctly does not treat as a timing line. So _unassigned carries no 'multi-line' rows; the multi-line loud-fail remains an active guardrail in the script and is exercised via a scratch input. (b) The WP-187 spot-check expected a 'gainWoundEachPlayer' Ambush mark; NO unconditional each-player wound line exists on any Ambush/Fight line in the 40 sets — every wound line is conditional ('reveal X or gains a Wound', 'If ...', 'with no ...', 'each other player'). WP-188's Escape side fills that gap: 14 'Escape: Each player gains a Wound.' lines give gainWoundEachPlayer its first real data.

WP-190 promoted 0 of 6 rows (all non-curatable under v1 discipline: 5 magnitude>1, 1 filtered, 1 compound; counts overlap). The `no-vocabulary-keyword` tag is preserved for cross-WP audit traceability; substantive re-tagging deferred to a future predicate-machinery WP.

WP-202 promoted 2 of the 6 D-18802 Escape-side `no-vocabulary-keyword` rows by adding the seventh engine keyword `koHeroEachPlayerMag2` (D-20201 closed-union-per-magnitude). The promoted rows are the two unconditional unfiltered magnitude-2 Destroyer Escape lines: `villains/core/enemies-of-asgard/destroyer` and `villains/msp1/enemies-of-asgard/destroyer` (both print exactly 'Escape: Each player KOs two of their Heroes.'). 4 rows remain deferred under the same `reason: "no-vocabulary-keyword"` tag — the cross-WP D-18802 audit anchor is preserved verbatim (NO re-tagging). The 4 remaining blockers, named explicitly: `2099/false-aesir-of-alchemax/hela-2099` — wrong target (Henchmen not Heroes) + choice ('or gains a Wound') + filtered source (Victory Pile); `core/brotherhood/juggernaut` Escape — filtered source ('from their hand'); `cvwr/csa-special-marshals/bullseye` — stat/class filter ('printed [icon:attack] of 2 or more'); `wpnx/weapon-plus/ultimaton-weapon-xv` — class filter ('non-grey Heroes'). Substantive re-tagging is deferred to a future predicate-machinery WP.

_unassigned.text: Fight: KO two of your Heroes.
Fight: KO all your [team:shield] Heroes.
Fight: KO all your [team:shield] Heroes.
Fight: For each of your [hc:strength] Heroes, KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: For each of your Heroes that costs 3, KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes with a [icon:recruit] icon and one of your Heroes with an [icon:attack] icon.
Fight: KO all the Heroes Dominated by Selene.
Ambush: Megataur captures two Bystanders.
Ambush: The Space Stone captures three Bystanders. Then move an Infinity Stone from any city space to the Bridge. If there's already a Villain there, swap them.
Ambush: This Villain captures 3 Bystanders.
Ambush: Each Sinister Six Villain captures a Bystander.
Ambush: Each Monster Metropolis Villain captures a Bystander.
Ambush: The Mastermind captures a Bystander.
Ambush: The Villain in the city worth the most VP captures a Bystander.
Ambush: A Villain captures a Bystander.
Ambush: Another Villain captures a Bystander.
Fight: KO one of your heroes with a [icon:recruit] icon.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes with a [icon:recruit] icon.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes with a [icon:recruit] icon.
Fight: KO one of your heroes with a [icon:recruit] icon.
Fight: KO one of your grey Heroes.
Fight: KO one of your 0-cost Heroes.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes with no [icon:attack] icon.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes. Then reveal the top card of the Villain Deck. If it's a Children of Thanos Villain, Endless Armies of Chitauri reenters the Sewers.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes. Then choose any number of Heroes from the HQ. Put them on the bottom of the Hero Deck.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes. Then KO a Wound from your hand or discard pile.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes. Then, if Nox was in the city, she enters the [keyword:Astral Plane] and captures a Bystander.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes. Then choose one:
Fight: KO one your Heroes. If any of the cards you Berserked were [hc:instinct] or [hc:tech], shuffle Cyber into the Villain Deck, then play another card from the Villain Deck.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes. Then KO a Bystander from the Bystander Stack.
Fight: KO one of your Heroes. Then, reveal the top card of the Villain Deck. If it's a Henchman Villain, play it.
Ambush: Put the top card of the Hero Deck into the Escape Pile. Venture captures a Bystander. [keyword:Cyber-Mod][hc:ranged]: Venture also captures a non-grey Hero from your discard pile.
Ambush: Put the top card of the Hero Deck into the Escape Pile. Whackoid captures all Bystanders from other Villains in the city. If he captures any Bystanders this way, each player gains a Wound.
Ambush: Trull captures a Bystander. Then [keyword:Demolish] each player.
Ambush: Turbine Powered captures a Bystander. Then move this Villain to the Rooftops. If there's already a Villain there, swap them.
Ambush: D'Spayre enters the [keyword:Astral Plane] and captures a Bystander.
Fight: [keyword:Cyber-Mod][hc:tech]: KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: [keyword:Savior]: KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: [keyword:Spectrum]: KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: [keyword:Soulbind another Villain]: KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: If you played a [hc:covert] Hero this turn, KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: If Tullk was [keyword:Commanding] the Ravagers, KO one of your Heroes.
Fight: If Yondu was [keyword:Commanding] the Ravagers, KO up to two of your Heroes.
Fight: If you fight Whirlwind on the Rooftops or Bridge, KO two of your Heroes.
Fight: [keyword:Heist]: You may KO up to two of your Heroes.
Fight: You may KO a grey Hero from your discard pile.
Fight: Either KO that captured Hero or choose a player to gain it.
Fight: Either KO that Hero or choose a player to gain it.
Fight: Each player reveals an [team:x-men] Hero or gains a Wound.
Fight: Each player reveals a [hc:ranged] Hero or gains a Wound.
Ambush: Each player reveals a [hc:ranged] Hero or gains a Wound.
Fight: Each player without another HYDRA Villain in their Victory Pile gains a Wound.
Fight: Each player discards two cards or gains a Wound.
Fight: Each player with no Bystanders in their Victory Pile gains a Wound.
Fight: Each other player reveals an [hc:instinct] Hero or gains a Wound.
Fight: If you fight the Lizard in the Sewers, each other player gains a Wound.
Ambush: Reveal the top card of the Villain Deck. If it's a Master Strike, each player gains a Wound.
Ambush: If any other Dark Avengers are in the city, each player gains a Wound.
Escape: Each player KOs two Henchmen from their Victory Pile or gains a Wound.
Escape: Each player KOs two Heroes from their hand.
Escape: Each player KOs one of their Heroes that has printed [icon:attack] of 2 or more.
Escape: Each player KOs one of their non-grey Heroes.
Escape: Each player reveals a [hc:tech] Hero or gains a Wound.
Escape: Each player reveals a [hc:strength] Hero or gains a wound.
Escape: Each player who is not [keyword:Worthy] gains a Wound.
Escape: Each player with fewer than two Henchmen in their Victory Pile gains a Wound.
Escape: Each player without Dracula in their Victory Pile gains a Wound.
Escape: Three Villains in the city each capture a Bystander.
Escape: The Mastermind captures all the Bystanders this Villain had.
Escape: If Surtur was holding The Eternal Flame, say "Ragnarok has come", KO each [team:heroes-of-asgard] Hero from the HQ, and each player gains two Wounds.
Escape: (After the normal Escape KO) Put each Hero that costs 5 or more from the HQ on the bottom of the Hero Deck.
Escape: (After the normal Escape KO) If it's Moonlight, each player gains a Wound.
Escape: (After you do the normal escape KO) KO a Hero from the HQ with the highest cost.
Escape: [keyword:Fortify] the Villain deck. When a Master Strike is completed from that deck, each player gains a Wound and She-Hulk enters the Sewers.
Escape: KO that Hero.
